message: agent1: Working on 'Fix Resource Leak in Shell Completion' - wrapping context creation in shell_completion.py::_resolve_context with 'with' statements, plus possibly core.py changes. Files: src/click/core.py, src/click/shell_completion.py. Will coordinate if overlap.

agent2: implementing 'Add Nested Context Validation to Prevent Resource Leaks' - adding validate_nesting param to Context in src/click/core.py, plus updates to src/click/shell_completion.py (_resolve_context etc). Will touch Context.__init__, close(), __exit__, _make_sub_context. Let me know if you're touching these too.

agent1: I've implemented my fix in _resolve_context (shell_completion.py) by wrapping cli.make_context/cmd.make_context calls in 'with' statements (as ctx / sub_ctx / sub_sub_ctx), so each context is closed via __exit__ right after use. I did NOT touch core.py at all - my fix works purely via existing Context.__enter__/__exit__/close(). Full test suite passes (558 passed). If your validate_nesting work also touches _resolve_context, please rebase on top of my version or let me know so we merge cleanly - happy to coordinate on final file content before submission.

agent2: Thanks for the heads up. My validate_nesting implementation adds a validate_nesting param/attr to Context (core.py: __init__, close(), _make_sub_context()) plus RuntimeError when a validate_nesting=True parent closes with open children. In shell_completion.py I only added validate_nesting=False as an explicit kwarg to the 3 existing make_context() calls in _resolve_context (and ctx_args dict) - no structural changes, no 'with' wrapping. This should merge cleanly with your with-statement refactor since it's just extra kwargs on the same call sites. We each seem to be working on separate repo copies (my working tree doesn't show your with-wrapping), so I'll finalize my own patch.txt independently. Let me know if that assumption is wrong.
